summary: Gruber follows up on viral attention to a scary-looking EU App Store warning shown on apps using external payment processing, clarifying that the warning has existed since March 2024 but went unnoticed because fewer than 100 developers use the option. He argues the disclosure itself is useful but its current design — a big red warning icon — is the problem. Apple proposed a neutral, informative redesign back in August 2024, but the European Commission told Apple to hold off on implementing changes and then never provided further guidance before fining Apple €500M for noncompliance.
key_points: The EU App Store external-purchase warning went viral despite being unchanged since March 2024, simply because so few apps trigger it
Apple proposed replacing the alarming red warning icon with a neutral gray info icon and clearer text back in August 2024
The European Commission told Apple not to implement its proposed improvements, then never followed up with guidance before issuing a €500M fine
Fewer than 100 developers have opted into the DMA's external purchase option, suggesting the framework is impractical
The disclosure concept is sound — users should know when purchases bypass Apple's IAP — but the execution looks deliberately hostile
money_quotes: I think this blew up a bit yesterday because, despite the fact that it had been around since March 2024, few of us had ever seen it before because so few apps in the App Store use it.
The problem with this disclosure, as it stands, is the way it looks: like a big scary warning.
It should be something more akin to the privacy 'nutrition label' information.
The EC raised no objection to the new disclosure proposal, but insisted that Apple not implement the changes at the time.
Heads, Apple was going to get fined by the EC. Tails, Apple was going to get fined by the EC.
key_insight: The European Commission blocked Apple from implementing its own proposed improvements to EU App Store disclosures, then fined Apple for the very noncompliance it prevented Apple from fixing.
